findings.detail: Shape: 1,234,580 rows × 156 columns
Date range: 2016-01-04 to 2026-06-26 (2,635 unique dates)
Ticker count: 476 (SPX universe)
Year distribution balanced: 2016-2025 ~115-120K rows each, 2026 partial 57.5K
144/156 columns have missing values; top: eps_dispersion 71.7%, qoq factors ~71%, analyst factors ~65%
2025-2026 PIT factor coverage: mean=98.9%, median=100.0% — excellent
76 PIT factors available (r_*, m_*, g_*, a_*, b_*, c_*, i_*)
1 PIT factor with <80% coverage in 2025-2026: c_buyback_yield (75.5%)
Legacy non-PIT factors properly excluded via EXCLUDE_COLS in optimization script
growth_composite = 0.5×fund_growth + 0.3×analyst + 0.2×income — matches claimed formula
Weight config: fund_ratio=0.70, growth_composite=0.30 — matches claimed config
Cross-sectional percentile ranking via scipy.stats.rankdata (method='average')
18 flip_factors defined (higher=better→lower=better inversions) — comprehensive list
No look-ahead bias detected: ranks computed per-date using only same-day data
Group ranks computed as equal-weighted mean of factor-level percentile ranks
Combo factors (quality_composite, safety_composite, growth_composite, etc.) use only group-level ranks
Walk-Forward properly splits train/test with rolling windows (no future data in training)
Final best: fr1+growth_composite0, Sharpe=1.876, MaxDD=-32.21%, CAGR=34.46%, WinRate=63.3%
Rank inversion (stability) passed: 0 negative recent windows, recent_avg=1.507 > threshold=0.930
RI metric is MISNAMED: 'rank_inversion' actually checks Sharpe degradation stability, NOT Top5% vs Bottom20% returns
Only 15/19 windows valid — 4 windows (W3-W6, 2018-H1 to 2020-H1) failed data quality due to growth_composite coverage <80% in 2018-2019
Early windows (W0-W2) avg Sharpe=3.101, Recent (W13-W15/W18) avg=1.507 → 51.4% degradation over time
Window sensitivity: only 6-month training passes RI (3/4/5/7/8 mo all fail RI) — very narrow optimal window
128 total candidates tested, 102 passed RI, 26 failed — systematic search performed
solo_growth_composite (Sharpe=1.58, RI=FAIL) and solo_fund_growth (Sharpe=1.416, RI=FAIL) — growth alone causes inversion; only combined with fund_ratio=0.70 does it pass
Comparison with V0.4.0: V0.4.1 Sharpe=1.876 vs V0.4.0 Sharpe=1.851 → +1.4% improvement (marginal)
14/15 valid windows have positive Sharpe (93.3% win rate across windows)
1 negative window: W7 (2020-H1) Sharpe=-0.262, MaxDD=-32.21% — COVID crash, expected but largest drawdown
Latest window W18 (2025-H2): Sharpe=2.266, MaxDD=-4.42%, WinRate=55% — recent performance strong
W17 (2025-H1): Sharpe=0.285 — weakest positive window, but still positive
Baseline comparison: V0.4.1 beats baseline in most windows (12/15 windows)
W13 (2023-H1): baseline Sharpe=2.766 > V0.4.1 Sharpe=1.238 — underperforms baseline in this window
W15 (2024-H1): baseline Sharpe=2.966 > V0.4.1 Sharpe=2.787 — slightly underperforms baseline
WinRate range: 44.2%-82.5%, most windows 55%-70% — reasonable
Trades per window: 40-43 (consistent top-10 rebalancing)
128 candidates tested with systematic grid search (solo groups, 2-factor combos, 3-factor finetune, multi-factor)
Candidate selection uses RI-passed pool first, falls back to all candidates — correct priority
CRITICAL: 'rank_inversion' metric is actually a Sharpe degradation stability check, NOT the standard Top5% vs Bottom20% rank inversion test
The actual Top5% > Bottom20% return test is NOT implemented — cannot verify signal direction correctness
growth_composite has only 15 valid windows vs 19 for other configs — 4 windows lost means survivorship bias in reported Sharpe
Window size sensitivity: RI only passes at exactly 6mo training. 3mo/4mo/5mo/7mo/8mo all fail — suggests overfitting to 6mo window
No look-ahead bias in factor computation — all PIT factors use reported financial data, ranks are cross-sectional per date
Train/test separation properly implemented with DateOffset-based rolling windows
backtest_engine dependency: not independently verified in this audit — results depend on its correctness
Cost=0.1% and stop_loss=-15% properly configured in Walk-Forward

overall.summary: V0.4.1 shows strong headline metrics (WF Sharpe=1.876, WinRate=63.3%) but has significant caveats that warrant caution. (1) The 'rank_inversion' metric is misnamed — it checks Sharpe stability, not actual Top5% vs Bottom20% return ordering. True rank inversion is untested. (2) 4 of 19 Walk-Forward windows were excluded due to growth_composite data coverage <80% in 2018-2019, introducing survivorship bias into the reported 15-window average. (3) Training window sensitivity is extreme — only the exact 6-month window passes RI, suggesting potential overfitting. (4) Early-to-recent Sharpe degradation is 51.4% (3.101→1.507), indicating model alpha may be decaying. (5) The improvement over V0.4.0 is marginal (+1.4%). Recommendation: Do NOT upgrade to V0.4.1 production without (a) implementing true Top5% vs Bottom20% rank inversion test, (b) ensuring growth_composite data coverage in 2018-2019, and (c) out-of-sample validation on 2026 data.
blockers: True rank inversion test (Top5% vs Bottom20%) not implemented — cannot confirm signal direction
4 windows excluded due to data quality — reported Sharpe may be inflated
warnings: 51.4% Sharpe degradation from early to recent windows
passes: No look-ahead bias detected in factor computation
